description: A designed composite card on an off-white paper-textured background. At the top, styled as a clipping from the conservative outlet, is the 'Washington Examiner' masthead (with its eagle/flag emblem) followed by a bold black serif headline: 'DHS and ICE deserve praise for arresting 5,000 illegal migrants in Los Angeles,' with byline 'By Christopher Tremoglie, August 27, 2025 12:58 pm.' Below the headline is a 3-row by 4-column grid of twelve photographs of arrested individuals - a mix of booking mugshots and candid arrest photos (several show people handcuffed beside officers in 'POLICE/ICE' tactical vests, one in a detainee transport van, one against a wall with a posted 'NO LOITERING / NO TRESPASSING' sign). The men and women depicted are of varied ages and ethnicities. The technique combines a flattering third-party op-ed headline with a 'rogues-gallery' grid of detainees to present a mass arrest as a praiseworthy success and to associate immigrants with criminality. Target audience: the public, reinforcing support for large-scale ICE enforcement. Tweet text: '5,000 illegal aliens detained in Los Angeles. The worst of the worst taken out of our neighborhoods: cartel members, rapists, pedophiles, murderers, drug dealers and more.'
notable_text: Washington Examiner | DHS and ICE deserve praise for arresting 5,000 illegal migrants in Los Angeles | By Christopher Tremoglie, August 27, 2025
